notes: Experiment 2a — delivery-report callback shape capture. Sent an unregistered-10DLC message (same sender + recipient as Experiment 1) with delivery_report="full" after configuring the Cloudflare Worker at sinch-webhook-receiver.joelnatkin.workers.dev as the Service Plan callback URL. Send returned HTTP 201 with a normal batch envelope. Phone did NOT receive the SMS — same silent carrier-side drop as Experiment 1. Sinch POST-ed a delivery-report callback to the Worker 1.769 seconds after the send response was issued, carrying batch_id correlation + a statuses[] array with code=310 / status="Failed". Key Phase 2 finding: the carrier-failure signal is delivered to Sinch (and onward to us) via callback in near-realtime (not the ~60s the procedure estimated) — overturns the Session 45 hypothesis that unregistered-10DLC drops might go unreported by the MNO. Phase 2 Session B's failure-detection strategy can be callback-primary with timeout fallback, not the other way around. api_timing_ms is null because curl -i was used without timing flags; send was observably fast but not precisely measured. Recipient phone number appears WITH leading + in the send request and WITHOUT it in both the send response and the callback payload — Phase 2 data layer must normalize. No signature / HMAC header on the callback (verification by header alone is not viable for Sinch XMS delivery reports; Phase 2 signature-verification design must rely on IP allowlist, mTLS, secret path segment, or a Sinch feature not enabled by default). Authorization header redacted as {SINCH_API_TOKEN}; no live credentials in this fixture.
